name: Costa Rica

colors.symbolism: the sky, opportunities, intellectual thought, perseverance and the eternity of God
clear thinking, happiness, wisdom, power, the beauty of the sky and peace
the warmth and generosity of the people, the blood spilt for freedom and the bravery of the nation

description: Five horizontal stripes in 1:1:2:1:1 proportion — blue, white, red, white, blue. The civil flag is the plain pentaband; the state flag adds the national coat of arms in a white oval positioned toward the hoist on the central red stripe.
history: Designed by Pacífica Fernández, wife of President José María Castro Madriz, the flag was adopted on 29 September 1848 following Costa Rica's break from the Federal Republic of Central America. The colours and pentaband layout are inspired by the French Tricolour, reflecting the ideals of the 1848 European revolutions. The current state-flag coat of arms was set by law in 1998, refining details of the volcanoes, ships and stars depicting the seven provinces.

features.symbolism: blue for the sky, intellectual thought and ideals of religious and political freedom; white for clear thinking, happiness, wisdom, power and beauty of the sky; red for the warmth and generosity of Costa Ricans, the blood spilt for freedom and the love of the Costa Rican people
